# minter ![](reference/figures/minter_logo.png)

The **minter** package is a collection of functions for calculating
effect sizes for meta-analysis of interactions.

The interaction can be between two factors (2-by-2 factorials) or
between a factor and time (repeated measures experiments). For both
design types, four effect size measures are available:

- lnRR: Log Response Ratio

- SMD: Standardized Mean Difference

- lnVR: Log Variability Ratio

- lnCVR: Log Coefficient of Variation Ratio

Typical effect sizes compare two values, the treatment vs. the control.
However, factorial experiments allow computing different contrasts (and
**minter** has functions for each):

- Individual effects: Direct comparisons between single treatments, the
  classic effect size.

- Main effects: Average effect of one factor across levels of another
  factor, analogous to the main effect in ANOVA.

- Interaction effects: How the effect of one factor depends on the level
  of another factor.

For repeated measures experiments, the effect size quantifies the
interaction between a factor and time.

See [References](https://fdecunta.github.io/minter/reference/index.html)
for more.

## Installation

From CRAN:

``` r

install.packages("minter")
```

From GitHub repo:

``` r

devtools::install_github("fdecunta/minter")
```

## Example

Suppose you need to estimate the effect size of the interaction between
fertilization × drought on plant biomass:

``` r

library(minter)

# Dummy data from 8 studies examining fertilization × drought on plant biomass
studies <- data.frame(
  study_id = 1:8,
  # Control: No fertilization, well-watered
  Ctrl_mean = c(12.3, 15.7, 10.8, 14.2, 11.9, 13.5, 16.1, 12.7),
  Ctrl_sd   = c(2.1, 3.2, 1.8, 2.7, 2.3, 2.9, 3.5, 2.4),
  Ctrl_n    = c(20, 24, 18, 22, 19, 25, 23, 21),
  # Fertilization only
  Fert_mean = c(18.5, 21.3, 16.2, 19.8, 17.1, 20.4, 22.7, 18.9),
  Fert_sd   = c(3.1, 4.1, 2.7, 3.6, 3.2, 3.8, 4.2, 3.4),
  Fert_n    = c(22, 25, 20, 24, 21, 26, 25, 23),
  # Drought only  
  Drought_mean = c(8.7, 11.2, 7.9, 10.1, 8.3, 9.8, 11.7, 9.4),
  Drought_sd   = c(1.8, 2.4, 1.6, 2.1, 1.9, 2.3, 2.6, 2.0),
  Drought_n    = c(19, 23, 17, 21, 18, 24, 22, 20),
  # Both treatments
  Both_mean = c(14.2, 17.8, 12.9, 16.3, 13.7, 16.1, 18.4, 15.2),
  Both_sd   = c(2.9, 3.7, 2.5, 3.3, 2.8, 3.4, 3.9, 3.1),
  Both_n    = c(21, 26, 19, 23, 20, 27, 24, 22)
)

# Calculate interaction effect: Does fertilization work differently under drought?
interaction_results <- lnRR_inter(
  data = studies,
  Ctrl_mean = "Ctrl_mean", Ctrl_sd = "Ctrl_sd", Ctrl_n = "Ctrl_n",
  A_mean = "Fert_mean", A_sd = "Fert_sd", A_n = "Fert_n", 
  B_mean = "Drought_mean", B_sd = "Drought_sd", B_n = "Drought_n",
  AB_mean = "Both_mean", AB_sd = "Both_sd", AB_n = "Both_n"
)

head(interaction_results)
#>   study_id Ctrl_mean Ctrl_sd ... yi        vi
#> 1        1      12.3     2.1     0.081  0.0069
#> 2        2      15.7     3.2     0.158  0.0068
#> 3        3      10.8     1.8     0.084  0.0073

# Meta-analysis
library(metafor)
res <- rma(yi, vi, ..., data = interaction_results)
```

## Acknowledgments

Thanks to Shinichi Nakagawa and Daniel Noble for generously sharing
their formulas.

# Package index

## SMD - Standardized Mean Difference

- [`SMD_ind()`](https://fdecunta.github.io/minter/reference/SMD_ind.md)
  : Individual effect: Standardized Mean Difference
- [`SMD_main()`](https://fdecunta.github.io/minter/reference/SMD_main.md)
  : Main effect: Standardized Mean Difference
- [`SMD_inter()`](https://fdecunta.github.io/minter/reference/SMD_inter.md)
  : Interaction effect: Standardized mean difference
- [`time_SMD()`](https://fdecunta.github.io/minter/reference/time_SMD.md)
  : Standardized Mean Difference: Interaction Between Treatment and Time

## lnRR - Log Response Ratio

- [`lnRR_ind()`](https://fdecunta.github.io/minter/reference/lnRR_ind.md)
  : Individual effect: Log Response Ratio
- [`lnRR_main()`](https://fdecunta.github.io/minter/reference/lnRR_main.md)
  : Main effect: Log Response Ratio
- [`lnRR_inter()`](https://fdecunta.github.io/minter/reference/lnRR_inter.md)
  : Interaction effect: Log Response Ratio
- [`time_lnRR()`](https://fdecunta.github.io/minter/reference/time_lnRR.md)
  : Log Response Ratio: Interaction Between Treatment and Time

## lnVR - Log Variation Ratio

- [`lnVR_ind()`](https://fdecunta.github.io/minter/reference/lnVR_ind.md)
  : Individual effect: Log of Variability Ratio
- [`lnVR_main()`](https://fdecunta.github.io/minter/reference/lnVR_main.md)
  : Main Effect: Log of the Variability Ratio
- [`lnVR_inter()`](https://fdecunta.github.io/minter/reference/lnVR_inter.md)
  : Interaction effect: Log Variability Ratio
- [`time_lnVR()`](https://fdecunta.github.io/minter/reference/time_lnVR.md)
  : Log of Variability Ratio: Interaction Between Treatment and Time

## lnCVR - Log Coefficient of Variation Ratio

- [`lnCVR_ind()`](https://fdecunta.github.io/minter/reference/lnCVR_ind.md)
  : Individual Effect: Log Coefficient Of Variation Ratio
- [`lnCVR_main()`](https://fdecunta.github.io/minter/reference/lnCVR_main.md)
  : Main Effect: Log Coefficient Of Variation Ration
- [`lnCVR_inter()`](https://fdecunta.github.io/minter/reference/lnCVR_inter.md)
  : Interaction Effect: Log Coefficient of Variation Ratio
- [`time_lnCVR()`](https://fdecunta.github.io/minter/reference/time_lnCVR.md)
  : Log Coefficient of Variation Ratio: Interaction Between Treatment
  and Time
